back_translation: Then Job answered:
'How long will you torment me and crush me with words?
Ten times now you have reproached me; you mistreat me shamelessly, without any shame.
Even if I have truly gone astray, my error is my own concern alone.
If you would indeed exalt yourselves over me and use my disgrace against me,
then know that God has treated me unjustly and has cast His net around me.
Behold, though I cry out, 'Violence!', no one answers; I call for help, but there is no justice.
He has blocked my way so I cannot pass, and He has covered my paths with darkness.
He has stripped me of my honor and removed the crown from my head.
He tears me down on every side until I am gone, and He uproots my hope like a tree.
His anger blazes against me, and He counts me as His enemy.
His troops advance together, build a siege ramp against me, and encamp around my tent.
He has made my brothers distant from me, and those I know have withdrawn from me entirely.
My kinsmen have forsaken me, and my close friends have forgotten me.
My houseguests and maidservants regard me as a stranger; I have become a foreigner in their sight.
I call my servant, but he does not answer, though I plead with him with my own mouth.
My breath is repulsive to my wife, and I am loathsome to the children of my own womb.
Even little children despise me; when I appear, they jeer at me.
All my closest friends abhor me, and those I love have turned against me.
My skin and flesh cling to my bones, and I have escaped only by a hair's breadth.
Have pity on me, have pity, O my friends, for the hand of God has touched me.
Why do you persecute me as God does? Will you never get enough of my flesh?
I wish that my words were recorded, that they were inscribed in a book,
with an iron stylus and lead, or chiseled in rock forever.
But as for me, I know that my Redeemer lives, and in the end He will stand upon the earth.
And after my skin has been destroyed, yet in my flesh I will see God.
I myself will see Him; my own eyes will behold Him, and not as a stranger. My heart faints with longing within me.
If you say, 'Let us persecute him, since the root of the matter is found in him,'
then you yourselves should fear the sword, for wrath brings punishment by the sword, so that you may know there is a judgment.'
